page: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is also in the top 10%, reaching 9076.97 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain strong in the top 25%. Single-core performance, measured by Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, falls into the average to strong range. Cryptographic and vector operations perform exceptionally well, as reflected in the top-tier Passmark encryption and extended instruction results, supported by hardware-level AES and VAES flags.

The absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff for I/O-heavy applications. However, the 50 Gbps network bandwidth helps mitigate external storage bottlenecks. The instance is highly capable for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching, where it achieves 129.17 million operations per second. It is also suitable for static web serving and moderate large language model prompt processing, though text generation speeds for larger models are average due to the lack of GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a balanced 4 GB of memory per core and a 50 Gbps baseline network speed, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and Redis operations. Conversely, single-core execution and complex LLM text generation fall into the average tier. This resource density makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized workloads, including software compilation, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving, where its massive core count can be fully utilized without the premium cost of specialized memory or compute-optimized hardware classes.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and 50 Gbps network speed.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A balanced, high-core-count virtual server delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and high-speed networking for enterprise workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Suited for multi-threaded databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
